title: Basic Science
description: Accessible science questions across chemistry, biology, physics, and astronomy.

questions.promptText: What is the smallest unit of an element?
questions.explanationText: An atom is the smallest unit that still retains the identity of an element.
pH measures how acidic or basic a substance is.

questions.promptText: Why does the sky usually look blue during the day?
questions.explanationText: Shorter blue wavelengths scatter more in the atmosphere, a process called Rayleigh scattering.

questions.promptText: What is magnetism fundamentally linked to?
questions.explanationText: Magnetism is tied to moving electric charges and the electromagnetic force.

questions.promptText: Roughly how much DNA is packed inside a single human cell if uncoiled?
questions.explanationText: A single human cell contains about two metres of DNA tightly packed into the nucleus.

questions.promptText: Which gas do humans need for respiration?
questions.explanationText: Humans need oxygen to support cellular respiration.

questions.promptText: What happens to many physics equations at the singularity of a black hole?
questions.explanationText: Current equations break down there, showing that our existing models are incomplete in that regime.
Evaporation is the process where a liquid changes into a gas.

questions.promptText: What is a catalyst used for in chemistry?
questions.explanationText: A catalyst speeds up a reaction without being consumed in the process.
options: To speed reactions without being used up
To add mass to a solution

questions.promptText: What best describes a black hole event horizon?
questions.explanationText: It is the boundary beyond which even light cannot escape the black hole's gravity.
